prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰), dual descriptor, and identification of most electrophilic (maximum f⁺) and most nucleophilic (maximum f⁻) atomic sites for benzaldehyde.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’; density fitting on by PySCF default).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz; single-point calculations for N, N+1, and N-1 electron states – no additional geometry optimization (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet for the N-electron state (charge 0, multiplicity 1). Cation and anion states computed at charge +1/−1 and doublet multiplicity (2).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend v <version not reported by JSON>).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states reported converged. The integrity block reports status "ok" and trustworthy = true. Warnings reproduced verbatim below. Sum of f⁺ and f⁻ equals 1.0000 (expected ≈1), so charge conservation checks pass. No additional convergence issues flagged.
